question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampArray()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a timestamps array where the values span a range larger than `maxRecordFileCloseIntervalNs` (10s in nanoseconds = 10,000,000,000ns), causing the upper bound `$3 = BigInt(maxTimestamp) + maxRecordFileCloseIntervalNs` to include many more record_file partitions than expected, amplifying the query cost and delaying block-time responses?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a block index of `0` to `getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromIndex`, and if the genesis block has a consensus_end far in the past, use the returned block data to falsely assert that a transaction is confirmed in a block whose timestamp is 500%+ of the average block time behind the current chain tip, misleading downstream consumers about network liveness?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampArray()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampArray` does not validate that the input timestamps array is actually sorted (only checks first vs last), by submitting a partially unsorted array that causes the inner loop's break condition to fire at the wrong position, silently omitting block associations for some timestamps and making those transactions appear unconfirmed?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `whereQuery` with both a `consensus_end >` and a `consensus_end <` filter that together define a range spanning the entire record_file history (e.g., `consensus_end > 0 AND consensus_end < MAX_BIGINT`), causing `getBlocks` to scan all partitions and return up to 100 rows, consuming maximum DB resources per request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampArray()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamps array with a single very large value (e.g., `9999999999999999999n`) so that `minTimestamp = maxTimestamp = 9999999999999999999n` and `$3 = 9999999999999999999n + 10000000000n`, causing the DB to scan all future partitions of the record_file table and return no rows, making the transaction appear to have no block and simulating an indefinite block delay?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the open-ended `WHERE consensus_end >= $1 ORDER BY consensus_end LIMIT 1` query in `recordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampQuery` by supplying a timestamp that falls in a gap between two record files (e.g., between blocks), causing the query to return the next block's data and making the transaction appear to belong to a block that started after the expected block time, simulating a block delay?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the `/api/v1/blocks/{hashOrNumber}` endpoint with requests using different single-character hash prefixes (0-9, a-f), causing `getByHashOrNumber` to execute concurrent LIKE queries against the record_file table, exhausting the DB connection pool and delaying block-time resolution for all users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampArray()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamps array where all values are `1n` (minimum nanosecond timestamp), causing `minTimestamp = 1n`, `maxTimestamp = 1n`, and `$3 = 1n + maxRecordFileCloseIntervalNs`, and the correlated subquery to scan from the genesis block forward, potentially returning the genesis block's data for all timestamps and making all transactions appear to belong to a block from the network's origin?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `whereQuery` with a `consensus_end` filter using a timestamp exactly equal to the current chain tip's `consensus_end`, causing `getBlocks` to return only the latest block, and by repeatedly querying with this exact value in a tight loop, exhaust DB connections and delay block-time reporting for other users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampArray()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `group by consensus_end` in the inner subquery of `recordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampArrayQuery` by submitting a timestamps array where many entries map to the same record file, causing the group-by to collapse them but the outer loop to still iterate over all timestamps, creating O(n*m) complexity in the JavaScript result-mapping loop and causing CPU exhaustion in the Node.js process?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a string timestamp that is not a valid nanosecond value (e.g., a very long numeric string) to `getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestamp`, causing the DB to spend time on type coercion or throw an error that is not properly handled, resulting in a null response that makes the transaction appear to have no block?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampArray()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamps array with values that span multiple record_file partitions (the table is partitioned by `consensus_end`), causing the correlated subquery to cross partition boundaries on every iteration of `unnest`, multiplying the number of partition scans and causing query latency proportional to the number of partitions crossed?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `whereQuery` with a `consensus_end >=` filter set to a timestamp just before the current chain tip, combined with `order=asc` and `limit=100`, causing `getBlocks` to return the 100 most recent blocks and by flooding with concurrent requests saturate the DB connection pool, delaying block-time responses for legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampArray()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getTimestampArrayContext` uses JavaScript comparison (`first > last`) on mixed types (e.g., string vs BigInt) in the timestamps array, causing incorrect order determination and wrong `minTimestamp`/`maxTimestamp` values, which then produce an invalid query window that excludes the correct record files and makes transactions appear unconfirmed?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromHash()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests to `getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromHash` with a hash prefix of `0x` (empty after stripping), causing the query to execute `WHERE hash LIKE '%'` and scan the entire record_file table on each request, exhausting DB I/O and delaying block-time lookups for all users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampArray()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamps array where the spread between `minTimestamp` and `maxTimestamp + maxRecordFileCloseIntervalNs` spans more than 24 hours of record files, causing the outer `WHERE consensus_end >= $2 AND consensus_end <= $3` to scan all record_file partitions for the past 24 hours, consuming DB resources and delaying block-time responses?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `whereQuery` with a `consensus_end` filter using a timestamp in the middle of the record_file history, combined with `order=desc` and `limit=100`, causing `getBlocks` to scan backwards through all partitions from that point, and by flooding with concurrent requests exhaust the DB connection pool and delay block-time reporting?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/recordFileService.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromTimestampArray()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of deduplication in the timestamps array before `unnest($1::bigint[])` expansion, by submitting an array with the same timestamp repeated thousands of times, causing the correlated subquery to execute redundantly for each duplicate and amplifying DB load proportionally to the number of duplicates?
